data.description: Arts Beacon is dedicated to providing exceptional art education for our students and career advancement and opportunities for our resident artists.

body.html:
Registration is open for Summer Camps and Workshops!

Check back soon for registration for adult summer classes.

title.text: Registration now open for Spring Session 4 and Summer Camp!

cards.class_tags: Start here on your creative journey in drawing

quick_description.text: Drawing is the foundation to all of our other classes! Wednesdays 6:00-8:00PM

description.html:
This class is for anyone who would like to explore the basics of drawing! Drawing is the structure beneath almost any creative endeavor, it’s an incredible meditative tool and is deeply grounding. This is a perfect class for beginners to start to find some tools as well as for more seasoned drawers to have a space to remember the basics and start fresh again. Some supplies included. A detailed supply list will be sent after sign up.

  • 8 week session
  • Time: Wednesdays 6:00-8:00PM
  • Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

cards.class_tags: Not so beginner anymore watercolor techniques

quick_description.text: Learn how to level up your watercolor techniques! Tuesdays 6:30-8:30PM

description.html:
Splash into some intermediate watercolor techniques! In this class you will explore your personal esthetic in watercolor paintings. We will play with form, tone, and design as we delve into composition and subject matter. We will examine materials, process, and design as you grow in skill and confidence with a community of learners who are all taking the journey together. Basic drawing skills are required, and some watercolor experience is preferred. 

A detailed supply list will be provided after signing up.

  • 8 week session
  • Time: Tuesdays 6:30-8:30PM
  • Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

quick_description.text: Beginning - intermediate level watercolor painting class for adults, Tuesdays 10AM-12PM

description.html:
Dive into the world of watercolors with our beginner-friendly class! Unleash your creativity as you learn about tone, design, and composition, and watch your skills and confidence flourish alongside a supportive community. Join us on this artistic journey today! A detailed supply list will be provided after sign up. 8 week session.

  • Time: Tuesdays 10AM-12PM

Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

quick_description.text: Explore different ways to be creative in this expressive class, Wednesdays 6:30-8:30PM

description.html:
This class is a beginning level, exploratory class that uses a variety of mediums to help each artist find their own best path of expression. We'll explore clay, collage, fiber arts, drawing and painting. Each class will explore how different materials give us a different doorway into creation. We'll get familiar with a variety of media and techniques, becoming more aware of what each medium offers, and utilize playfulness and experimentation to allow a more open ended form of creation. Ultimately this class is useful for students that want to try new mediums or are hoping to get more clarity on which mediums best serve their artistic interest.

Most supplies included. You will need a mixed media sketchbook. 8 week session.

Wednesdays 6:30-8:30

Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

cards.class_tags: Playful expressive painting class for adults

quick_description.text: Find your expressive voice in this engaging class, Thursdays 6:30-8:30PM

description.html:
This is a beginning to intermediate class for learning how to engage with colors confidently in your paintings and develop an understanding for how and why you like to use them. This will be a class rooted in play, non judgment and finding expression from your unique voice. You will learn how to let go and trust the process while mixing colors, gaining understanding of various painting techniques and how to develop compositions. 8 week session.

*Suggested (not mandatory) prerequisite Beginning Drawing

A detailed supply list will be provided.

  • Time: Thursdays 6:30-8:30
  • Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

quick_description.text: Beginning to intermediate clay hand building class, Thursdays 6:30-8:30PM

description.html:
Coil, Pinch, Slab Pottery Hand Building is a beginner to intermediate level class exploring hand-built functional pottery in a relaxed environment. Make Cups, bowls and plates and bring a creative eye to everyday objects. Supplies for this class can be purchased at your local clay store. A detailed supply list will be provided. 8 week session

Students are eligible to come to open studio time throughout the semester. Open studio is available on Sundays from 10-2:00PM.

  • Time: Thursdays 6:30-8:30PM
  • Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

quick_description.text: Fun and supportive art class, Thursdays 3:30-4:30PM

description.html:
Designed for kids in 1st-3rd grade that want to build up their confidence as artists. This drawing, painting, printmaking and sculpting class is full of experimenting, exploration and movement. We will focus on process-based art with an emphasis in exploring materials and developing a strong visual aesthetic by experimenting with color, line, texture, and 2-D and 3-D design. Students will learn to appreciate their artistic differences and celebrate their successes. Dress to get messy! 8 week class. All supplies included.

  • Time: Thursdays 3:30-4:30
  • Now registering for
  • Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

cards.class_tags: Kids mixed media class for 3rd-5th graders

quick_description.text: Playful and supportive, process based art class, Thursdays 3:30-4:30PM

description.html:
Drawing, painting, printmaking, clay and mixed media class for 3rd-5th graders that uses a process based approach to teaching but with more emphasis on developing technique. Students will be given instruction in observational drawing, color mixing, print making and clay hand building techniques. All Supplies included. 8 week session.

  • Time: Thursdays 3:30-4:30
  • Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

cards.class_tags: Tween art in various 2D and 3D media

description.html:
The Dragon group is designed specifically for kids in middle school - grades 6th-7th (and 8th if they aren't quite ready for the Teen Portfolio class).

The most pivotal time in a young artists life is between the ages 11-15. Our Tween students get a lot of instruction and encouragement to take their art to the next level in a supportive environment. We will be exploring observational drawing, watercolor, acrylic painting, clay sculptural and functional pottery to create amazing masterpieces! All supplies included

  • Time: Wednesdays 4:30-5:30
  • Now registering for

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

cards.class_tags: Teen art class for portfolio building

quick_description.text: Perspective Drawing, Still Life Drawing, Figure Drawing, Sculpture, Figure Painting, Clay Pottery, and Painting for 8th-12th graders, Thursdays 5:00-6:15PM

description.html:
This class is for teens that want to improve their art and start building up their art portfolio for college applications, grants and scholarships. We will rotate through drawing, painting, and clay working toward getting well thought out and completed portfolio work. For the 2025-2026 school year our project blocks will cover Perspective Drawing, Still Life Drawing, Figure Drawing, Figure Sculpture, Figure Painting, Clay Pottery, and Painting. All teens that take classes will have the opportunity to work as a summer assistant or intern. All supplies included. 8 week session.

  • Time: Thursdays 5:00-6:15

Spring Session 4 - March 17th- May 17th

cards.class_tags: Mixed media, drawing, painting, clay camps

quick_description.text: Summer camp for kids in rising 1st-3rd grade! Different theme each week!

description.html:
This summer we are offering 7 different weeks of camps for rising 1st-3rd graders. "Art Explorers" is a mixed media camp for our youngest students! Camps run from 9AM-1PM and will incorporate painting, drawing and sculpting. Each session will have different projects and themes so campers will have a different approach to art making each week of camp. We will have one teacher and one assistant and the camp will be capped at 8 students for a 4/1 ratio. Campers will need to bring a lunch. All the art supplies needed and a snack will be provided.

Session 1 June 1-5 Glitter and Sparkle

Session 2 June 8-12 Extraordinary Animals

Session 3 June 15-19 Magic and Mythology

Session 4 June 22-26 All About Me

Session 5 July 6-10 Creature Feature

Session 6 July 13-17 Our Natural World

Session 7 July 20-24 Forts and Castles

cards.class_tags: Mixed media, drawing, painting, clay camps

description.html:
This summer we are offering 7 weeks of camps for rising 3rd-6th graders. There are 4 different themes to choose from. Clay Play, Book Making, Painting and Drawing and Mixed Media. Camps run from 9AM-1PM. Each session will have different projects and themes so campers will have a different approach to art making each week of camp. We will have one teacher and one assistant and the camp will be capped at 8 students for a 4/1 ratio. Campers will need to bring a lunch. All the art supplies needed and a healthy snack will be provided.

Camp runs Monday - Friday from 9AM-1PM.

June 1st-5th Clay Play

June 8th-12th Book Arts

June 15th-19th Caly Play

June 22nd-26th Mixed Media

July 6th-10th Drawing and Painting

July 13th-17th Book Arts

July 20th-24th Drawing & Painting

title.text: CAMP: Summer Teen and Tween Intensives

cards.class_tags: Clay, Book Arts, Drawing and Painting

description.html:
This summer we are offering higher level intensive classes for teens and tweens who want to build their skills in a supportive and focused environment. Each class meets from 2-4PM on Monday-Friday of the assigned week.

For the Tweens (rising 6th-8th grades), we are offering a Book Arts class that will explore the fun of making your own books through a variety of mediums as well as creating serialized content such as comics. In our painting & drawing intensive, we will be exploring observational drawing, watercolor, and acrylic painting techniques.

For the Teens (rising 9th-12th grades), we are offering a Ceramics class that focuses on a variety of hand-building techniques from pinch pots to slabs to coils. In our Painting & Drawing intensive, we will be exploring drawing and painting still lives in charcoal, acrylic and watercolor. 

 The summer intensives will run Monday-Friday from 2-4PM. All the supplies are included. 

Tweens - Book Arts June 8th-12th 2-4PM

Teens - Clay June 15th-19th Monday-Friday 2-4PM

Tweens - Drawing & Painting July 6th-10th 2-4PM

Teens - Drawing & Painting July 13th-17th Monday-Friday 2-4PM

quick_description.text: Friday adult workshop to connect and unwind - bring your own refreshments. May 8, 6:30-8:30PM

description.html:
In honor of Mother’s Day, learn how to create a charming watercolor illustration for an important woman in your life. Materials will be provided - just bring a willingness to learn and have fun! Bring-your-own refreshments are welcome.

Friday, May 8,6:30-8:30pm

CLASS FILLED! Check back for the next one in June

Clancy Provence is an Atlanta-based watercolor illustrator whose personalized portraits capture the people she loves through whimsical detail and warm, expressive storytelling.

quick_description.text: Bring a child (ages 6-10) for some special creative time! May 9, 1-2:30PM

description.html:
In this workshop, each adult+child duo will create a multimedia flower garden. We will use a variety of materials and techniques to create one-of-a-kind patterned paper designs. We will then transform the patterned papers into bright, multilayer flowers, which will nestle into a flower garden. These collaborative art pieces make great Mother’s Day gifts! All materials will be provided.

Saturday, May 9, 1-2:30pm

Shannon Kelly, co-owner of Arts Beacon, is a long-time educator and watercolor artist. She loves to help students of all ages to find joy and connection through process-based art projects.

quick_description.text: Create a unique flower arrangement May 9, 3-5PM

description.html:
In this workshop you will create a spring centerpiece using all compostable or reusable components. Each participant will learn how to create a balanced design using easily found greenery and flowers. You will have a beautiful piece to enjoy at the end of the class and the possibility to remake and enjoy it many times.

Saturday, May 9, 3-5pm

Patricia Daniel is an Atlanta-based floral designer doing event work, workshops, and installations. She studied internationally at the Boerma Institute and at Blomatelier (the Netherlands), Tywla’s Flower Farm (Canada), and with Hitomi Gilliam (Vancouver, Japan, and Charleston). Her interests are sustainable flower design and creating long lasting structures for large installations.

title.text: Stay informed about studio events or class changes!

body.html: Our Resident Gallery is open on Sundays 10-2PM

image_captions.text: I think I have found my inner artist using charcoal... I would have never known if it hadn't been for Jena.
"I ❤️ Arts Beacon. I don’t consider myself ‘arty’ but Jena made it awesome - her vibe of relaxed, cheerful guidance encouraged me to have a wonderful time."
"I absolutely recommend Arts Beacon. Jena is wonderful with children, and I will not hesitate to send own children and clients to her!"
